data.title: DeFi perps volume explodes past $1T in a record month so far
data.description:
Decentralized perpetual trading volume is set for a massive month in October, having just passed a record $1 trillion with a week still to spare as traders place big bets on the crypto markets.

The $1 trillion milestone has already beaten August’s record of $762 billion by a considerable margin, according to data from DeFiLlama.

While Hyperliquid leads October with $317.6 billion in trading volume, Lighter, Aster, and edgeX have also fared well, seeing $255.4 billion, $177.6 billion and $134.7 billion, respectively, with the rest made up of smaller decentralized exchanges.

Those platforms contributed to a record $78 billion in decentralized perps volume on Oct. 10 alone. 

Given the current run rate, decentralized perps volume is on track to finish October at about $1.3 trillion — nearly doubling August’s tally.

Perps have become popular due to their 24/7 trading, high leverage, no expiration, and the ability to profit from both rising and falling markets — attracting speculative traders seeking higher returns with minimal holding requirements.

CEXs are still perps kings, but DEXs are catching up

Decentralized perps trading volume remains a fraction of that seen on centralized exchanges, with Binance and Bybit seeing $69.3 billion and $26 billion in trading volume over the last 24 hours, CoinGecko data shows.

However, that lead is narrowing hard and fast as crypto innovators continue to build more user-friendly frontends for crypto perp traders to interact with.

Hyperliquid was the breakthrough protocol, Infinex founder says

Decentralized perps platforms have been around for nearly a decade, led earlier by the likes of Synthetix, dYdX, and GMX. However, Hyperliquid was the first one to “get it right” and scale successfully, Infinex founder Kain Warwick told Cointelegraph earlier this month.

One of the most widely used crypto wallets, MetaMask, integrated Hyperliquid on Oct. 8, letting users access Hyperliquid’s perpetual swaps exchange directly through its app.

data.title: Nidec Corp. Shares Fall Sharply After It Retracts Earnings Guidance
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

Nidec Corp. shares fell sharply after it retracted its earnings forecasts and decided not to pay an interim dividend, citing ongoing investigations into suspected accounting irregularities.

Shares were 6.4% lower at 2,387.0 yen, equivalent to $15.64, on Friday morning in Tokyo.

The Japanese maker of electric motors and other equipment said Thursday that its revenue and profit projections for the fiscal year ending March 2026 were undetermined and that it would announce them when it becomes possible to do so. Nidec also canceled a share buyback program announced in May.

The Kyoto-based manufacturer set up a third-party committee in September to investigate potential cases of improper accounting. The company said then that earlier probes had uncovered documents suggesting that the company might have engaged in improper accounting, including the potential cases related to the timing of asset write-downs.

Nidec had earlier projected revenue of Y2.600 trillion and net profit of Y200.00 billion for the fiscal year ending March 2026. It had planned to distribute an interim dividend of Y20 a share and buy back up to Y35.0 billion of its own shares under the repurchase program.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: Japan Flash PMI Signals Further Slowdown in Private Sector — Market Talk
data.description: Flash PMI data for Japan points to a further loss of momentum across the private sector in October, says Annabel Fiddes, economics associate director at S&P Global Market Intelligence. Foreign demand for Japanese goods and services fell again, and new business declined for the first time in 16 months, S&P data show. While the service sector remained a key growth engine for the wider economy, fading momentum will be a point of concern to monitor in the coming months, Fiddes says. Manufacturing production continued to contract, but manufacturers were more upbeat about the year ahead than service providers. Many hope a recovery in global economic conditions, new product releases and stronger demand for electronics will help boost output, she adds.(fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

data.title: Alaska Air's Q3 revenue grows 23% YoY, hitting record high
data.description:
Overview

  • Alaska Air Group's Q3 2025 revenue reaches record $3.77 bln, up 23% YoY

  • Adjusted EPS for Q3 was $1.05, within prior expectations of $1 to $1.40

  • The carrier launched Atmos Rewards loyalty program, exceeding premium credit card sign-up goals

Outlook

  • Company expects Q4 unit revenue to increase by low single digits YoY

  • Alaska Air projects Q4 capacity growth of 2%-3% from last year

  • Company forecasts Q4 EPS of at least $0.40 and full-year EPS of at least $2.40

Result Drivers

  • REVENUE GROWTH - Record Q3 revenue driven by increased corporate travel and strong close-in demand

  • LOYALTY PROGRAM SUCCESS - Atmos Rewards launch exceeded premium credit card sign-up expectations

  • COST INCREASES - Higher fuel costs and elevated unit costs due to IT outage and challenging weather conditions

data.title: Canada caps how many US-made vehicles Stellantis and GM can import tariff-free
data.description:
Canada has sharply reduced tariff-free import quotas for General Motors and Stellantis , citing their decisions to scale back manufacturing in the country, the country's Department of Finance said in a statement on Thursday.

GM's annual remission quota is cut by 24.2%, and Stellantis' by 50%, following their failure to meet commitments under Canada's auto remission framework, the statement said.

In April, Canada said it would impose 25% counter-tariffs on all vehicles imported from the United States that are not compliant with the U.S.-Mexico-Canada trade deal.

However, the Canadian finance ministry later said that auto manufacturers will be allowed to import a certain number of U.S.-assembled, free-trade-compliant vehicles if they continue to produce cars locally and complete planned investments.

The government statement, issued by Finance and National Revenue Minister Francois-Philippe Champagne and Industry Minister Melanie Joly, criticized GM for reducing production at its Oshawa and Ingersoll facilities in Ontario, and Stellantis for canceling production plans at its Brampton assembly plant.

"These unacceptable decisions are in contravention of their legal obligations to Canada and Canadian workers," Champagne said, emphasizing the government's resolve to protect the auto sector.

Stellantis announced plans to move its Jeep Compass production from Ontario to the U.S., and General Motors ended BrightDrop van output in Ontario, citing weaker demand.

GM and Stellantis did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment.

The policy shift was first reported by CBC News.

data.title: OP Bancorp reports results for the quarter ended September 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• OP Bancorp reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 45 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended September 30, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 36 cents. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 44 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 18% to $24.48 million from a year ago; analysts expected $24.25 million.

  • OP Bancorp's reported EPS for the quarter was 45 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $6.7 million.

  • OP Bancorp shares had fallen by 2.4% this quarter and lost 14.1% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 7.8% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the banks peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for OP Bancorp is $16.00, about 15.1% above its last closing price of $13.58

data.title: South Korea stocks head for fourth weekly gain at record high
data.description:
Key points:
  • KOSPI rises, foreigners net buyers
  • Korean won strengthens against dollar
  • South Korea benchmark bond yield rises

Round-up of South Korean financial markets:

** South Korean shares rose on Friday and were set to log a fourth weekly gain at a record high level as a planned meeting between Donald Trump and Xi Jinping, as well as a summit between Washington and Seoul, raised hopes to ease trade tensions. The won strengthened, while the benchmark bond yield rose.

** The benchmark KOSPI was up 61.47 points, or 1.60%, at 3,907.03 as of 00:52 GMT. For the week, the index is up 4.2%.

** Among index heavyweights, chipmaker Samsung Electronics rose 1.45%, while peer SK Hynix gained 5.22%. Battery maker LG Energy Solution climbed 3.02%.

** U.S. President Donald Trump will meet Chinese President Xi Jinping next week as part of a trip to Asia, the White House said on Thursday.

** A trade war simmering for months exploded into full view in early October, after Beijing dramatically expanded curbs on exports of rare earths, in response to a U.S. hike in the number of firms blocked from purchases of its technology.

** South Korea's finance ministry said on Friday the government will deploy measures to stabilize the forex market if needed, as volatility in the dollar-won market continues.

** Hyundai Motor was up 0.10% and sister automaker Kia Corp fell 0.17%, respectively. Steelmaker POSCO Holdings added 3.57%, while drugmaker Samsung BioLogics rose 1.38%.

** Of the total 926 traded issues, 424 shares advanced, while 419 declined.

** The won was quoted at 1,436.0 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.10% higher than its previous close at 1,437.4.

** The KOSPI has risen 62.83% so far this year.

** The won has strengthened 2.5% against the dollar so far this year.

** The most liquid three-year Korean treasury bond yield (KR3YT=RR) rose 0.3 basis points to 2.606%, while the benchmark 10-year yield gained 0.9 bps to 2.921%.

Eric Richard Remer, Chief Executive Officer and Director of EverCommerce, sold 19,200 shares of Common Stock on October 21 and 22, 2025, for a total sale amount of $219,451. The sales were executed at weighted average prices of $11.4357 and $11.4204 per share, respectively, across multiple transactions. Following these transactions, Remer directly owns 3,011,924 shares and indirectly owns 6,212,662 shares of EverCommerce through various trusts and partnerships. All transactions were made under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established on June 12, 2025.

data.title: Singapore Q3 Private Home Prices Revised Downward
data.description:
Private home prices in Singapore rose 0.9% quarter-on-quarter in Q3 2025, revised down from the preliminary estimate of 1.2% and below the 1.0% increase in the previous quarter.

This marked the fourth consecutive quarter of growth, albeit at a softer pace.

The moderation was most evident in landed property prices, which rose 1.4% compared with 2.2% in Q2.

data.title: China Stock Market May Add To Its Winnings On Friday
data.description:
BEIJING (dpa-AFX) - The China stock market ticked higher again on Thursday, one day after halting the two-day winning streak in which it had advanced more than 75 points or 1.9 percent. The Shanghai Composite Index now sits just above the 3,920-point plateau and it may see additional support on Friday.

The global forecast for the Asian markets is positive, with oil and technology stocks expected to lead the markets higher. The European and U.S. markets were up and the Asian bourses are expected to open in similar fashion.

The SCI finished modestly higher on Thursday as gains from the financials and oil companies were capped by weakness from the property sector.

For the day, the index added 8.65 points or 0.22 percent to finish at 3,922.41 after trading between 3,877.53 and 3,926.22. The Shenzhen Composite Index rose 5.38 points or 0.22 percent to end at 2,457.90.

Among the actives, Industrial and Commercial Bank of China advanced 1.03 percent, while Bank of China gained 0.55 percent, Agricultural Bank of China slumped 1.24 percent, China Merchants Bank added 0.69 percent, Bank of Communications collected 0.84 percent, China Life Insurance strengthened 1.23 percent, Jiangxi Copper perked 0.03 percent, Aluminum Corp of China (Chalco) accelerated 3.31 percent, Yankuang Energy improved 0.75 percent, PetroChina spiked 3.15 percent, China Petroleum and Chemical (Sinopec) climbed 1.09 percent, Huaneng Power rallied 2.25 percent, China Shenhua Energy rose 0.19 percent, Gemdale tanked 2.63 percent, Poly Developments dropped 0.92 percent and China Vanke was unchanged.

The lead from Wall Street is upbeat as the major averages opened slightly higher and trended generally to the upside throughout the session.

The Dow climbed 144.20 points or 0.31 percent to finish at 46,734.61, while the NASDAQ jumped 201.40 point or 0.89 percent to close at 22,941.80 and the S&P 500 added 39.04 points or 0.58 percent to end at 6,738.44.

The slow start on Wall Street followed mixed corporate earnings news as companies like Tesla (TSLA) and IBM Corp. (IBM) disappointed but Honeywell (HON) beat the street.

Crude oil surged on Thursday, extending gains from the past two sessions, as the U.S. imposed sanctions against Russian oil majors. West Texas Intermediate crude for December delivery was up $3.22 or 5.50 percent at $61.72 per barrel.

Reflecting the strength in the energy sector, the Philadelphia Oil Service Index spiked 4.8 percent and the NYSE Arca Oil Index jumped 3.0 percent.

Substantial strength was also visible among computer hardware stocks, as reflected by the 4.0 percent surge by the NYSE Arca Computer Hardware Index. Networking, semiconductor and steel stocks also saw considerable strength, while transportation stocks moved to the downside.

data.title: Kasikornbank Target Price Raised to THB200.00 From THB192.00 by UOB Kay Hian >KBANK.TH
Yomiuri: Japan Leads in 'Nature Positive' Initiatives; Nippon Life Promotes Own Indicator for Investment
data.description:
By Tomoko Echizenya

Dow Jones Yomiuri Shimbun Pro Managing Editor

Japan is leading the world in "nature positive" management aimed at halting and reversing the loss of nature.

The nation boasts the world's highest number of companies that disclose assessments of their impact on nature in financial reports. Nippon Life Insurance Co., one of Asia's leading institutional investors by assets under management, this summer released its own methodology on how to evaluate corporate efforts aimed at nature restoration and incorporate those evaluations into investment and financing decisions.

Despite challenges for the advancement of sustainability management, due to such factors as the current U.S. administration's negative stance toward decarbonization, Nippon Life plans to accelerate its nature restoration support initiatives.

"Even if (companies) are reluctant (to implement) climate change measures, the idea of protecting both nature and primary industries is easier to accept. Market sentiment isn't unfavorable," said Yasutoshi Miyamoto, general manager of the company's Responsible Investment Strategy Office, during an interview with The Yomiuri Shimbun.

Attracting investment key to success

The goal of achieving a transition to a nature-positive society by 2030 was agreed upon at the 15th Conference of the Parties to the U.N. Convention on Biological Diversity in 2022.

Japan currently leads the world with 206 companies having adopted the Taskforce on Nature-related Financial Disclosures framework, which quantitatively visualizes the relationship between corporate activities and natural capital. There are over 600 such companies globally.

A key driver for accelerating these efforts is nature finance.

While greenhouse gas emissions provide a clear metric for climate change, evaluation metrics have yet to be established regarding the restoration of natural capital and biodiversity due to their complexity. Nippon Life this summer unveiled its "Nature Finance Approach" regarding nature restoration.

"Its defining feature is that it simplifies complex scientific metrics to make them applicable to corporate practice," said Miyamoto.

The approach aims to broaden corporate engagement by being applicable not only to industries directly handling natural capital such as food, agriculture and forestry, but also to a wide range of sectors including real estate and IT.

Assessing benefits, risks

Nippon Life has set the ultimate goal of seeing indicators of global activities fall within the "Planetary Boundaries," which represent the sustainable limits of human activities from the viewpoint of environmental change. It uses indicators such as Net Primary Production (NPP), Human Appropriation of Net Primary Production (HANPP) and the populations of key species at a site, including endangered species.

NPP is the net amount of carbon retained in an ecosystem through plant photosynthesis. HANPP is the proportion of NPP used by humans.

The approach will check if a company's activities lead to benefits as seen through increases in NPP or species populations, or decreases in HANPP. It will also check if there are risks based on the Do No Significant Harm (DNSH) principle, which include the possibility of soil or water contamination, the introduction of invasive alien species or local communities suffering disadvantages.

Consider electronics manufacturer A, which produces and sells PCs, recovers a total of 15 tons of the rare metal cobalt annually from 300,000 PCs. Assuming cobalt mining destroys an average of 0.01 hectares of forest per ton, the recovery contributes to a 0.15-hectare reduction in annual forest destruction. Multiplying this by the NPP per unit area — which varies based on the type of forest, but in this calculation is 12 tons of carbon per hectare — the project can be evaluated as reducing HANPP by 1.8 tons a year.

If no risks are found, the target for investment or financing would be judged "eligible."

Widespread application called for

Nippon Life has set a cumulative investment and financing target of 5 trillion yen for "thematic investments" linked to Sustainable Development Goals and other initiatives from fiscal 2017 through fiscal 2030. As of the end of the previous fiscal year in March, it had invested about 3.3 trillion yen.

The company plans to actively advance investments and financing using its new approach going forward.

However, discussions on standardizing evaluation methods are still in their early stages. "We intend to continue refining this approach through discussions with the (Japanese) government and relevant organizations," said Miyamoto. The concept of "nature positive" itself has not yet been sufficiently recognized, and has challenges in that it is hard to understand and issues vary by region.

"It will be necessary to have quantitative international coordination like the Paris Agreement on climate change," Miyamoto said. "Another key element is market-making. We must create a change where engaging in nature restoration becomes a business unto itself and transforms industrial structures," he said, adding, "We haven't reached that stage yet."

Other institutions including Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Bank Ltd. and Mizuho Financial Group Inc. are also advancing initiatives regarding nature finance.

----

This article is from The Yomiuri Shimbun. Neither Dow Jones Newswires, MarketWatch, Barron's nor The Wall Street Journal were involved in the creation of this content.

YDN-M0000152569-1

data.title: Japan's century-old Fujikura rides AI data centre boom to become Nikkei standout
data.description:
Key points:
  • Shares in optical fibre maker up 160% since start of 2025
  • Founded in 1885, Fujikura started out making silk and insulated wires
  • Investors looking for next batch of winners in AI supply chain

By Junko Fujita

Japan's Fujikura has become a standout in the red-hot Nikkei share gauge on expectations the century-old wire maker will benefit from increased investment in artificial intelligence data centres that use its optical fibres.

The firm got another leg up this week, rising 6% after Sanae Takaichi was elected prime minister following pledges of targeted investment in AI and other key industries.

Since the start of 2025, shares in Fujikura are up more than 160%, outpacing the Nikkei's 22% advance to an all-time high. Its market value of about $33 billion now rivals that of better-known Japanese manufacturers such as air-conditioning maker Daikin and construction equipment manufacturer Komatsu .

Ups and downs in global equities have been increasingly tied to the fortunes of the so-called "Magnificent Seven" U.S. technology companies at the forefront of AI development, including Nvidia , Amazon , and Microsoft .

In Japan, that euphoria has filtered down to major suppliers and investors in the AI sector, such as Advantest , Tokyo Electron and SoftBank Group . Those firms alone account for most of the Nikkei's gain since early September.

Fujikura, founded in 1885 during Japan's Meiji Era of modernisation, has been a focus for investors searching through the AI supply chain to find the next batch of winners.

"Generative AI is a big theme and key driver for the Japanese market," said Kazuaki Shimada, chief strategist at IwaiCosmo Securities. "Now investors have started looking for new targets that may become the next Fujikura."

Mitsui Kinzoku , which makes materials for data centre servers, is another AI-related high-flyer in the Japanese market, jumping 192% so far in 2025. Another supplier JX Advanced Metals has soared fourfold since its market debut in March.

Starting out as a maker of silk and insulated wires, Fujikura went on to produce the world's first optical fibre in 1959. Those fibres are now a key component of AI data centres, with about 75% of the company's output going to overseas customers, such as Google owner Alphabet .

A spokesperson said Fujikura had added capacity for fibre production at its existing facility in February, and it announced in August it would spend 45 billion yen ($298.45 million) to add another factory.

($1 = 150.7800 yen)

data.title: Newmont Mining Corp (Holding Co) Profit Climbs In Q3
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Newmont Mining Corp (Holding Co) (NEM.OB) revealed earnings for its third quarter that Increases, from last year

The company's bottom line totaled $1.832 billion, or $1.67 per share. This compares with $922 million, or $0.80 per share, last year.

Excluding items, Newmont Mining Corp (Holding Co) reported adjusted earnings of $1.883 billion or $1.71 per share for the period.

The company's revenue for the period rose 20.0% to $5.524 billion from $4.605 billion last year.

Newmont Mining Corp (Holding Co) earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: $1.832 Bln. vs. $922 Mln. last year.

-EPS: $1.67 vs. $0.80 last year.

-Revenue: $5.524 Bln vs. $4.605 Bln last year.
